NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND A DOWNBURST KNOCKED DOWN SEVERAL LARGE TREE LIMBS AND A FENCE ALONG CABOT ROAD. PATIO FURNITURE FROM TWO HOMES ALONG CABOT ROAD WAS BLOWN INTO ADJACENT FIELDS. OTHER TREE LIMBS WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG HIGHWAY 29 AT LAMAR ROAD. ALL DAMAGE WAS IN A NORTHWEST TO SOUTHEAST DIRECTION.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND MICROBURST WIND DAMAGE ALONG A 2 MI BY 1/4 MI PATH ALONG GAUTHIER ROAD EAST OF LAKE CHARLES REGIONAL APT. ONE MOBILE HOME HAD MAJOR ROOF DAMAGE IN THE QUAIL RIDGE PARK. A CHURCH LOST SIDING ON TOM HEBERT RD AT GAUTHIER RD WHILE SEVERAL MOBILE HOMES HAD MINOR ROOF AND SKIRTING DAMAGE. A LARGE SIGN WAS BLOWN DOWN AT CORBINA RD. SEVERAL LARGE TREE LIMBS WERE DOWNED ALONG THE PATH. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 70 MPH.

PUBLIC REPORTED BROKEN WINDOWS AT QUAIL RIDGE MOBILE HOME PARK LOCATED ON GAUTHIER ROAD APPROXIMATELY 2 MILES EAST OF LAKE CHARLES REGIONAL AIRPORT. HAIL OF UNKNOWN SIZE WAS ALSO REPORTED AT THIS LOCATION.
QUARTER SIZED HAIL REPORTED ON CORBINA ROAD APPROXIMATELY 2 MILES EAST OF THE LAKE CHARLES REGIONAL AIRPORT.
TREE DOWN ON HOKE ROAD...BETWEEN ROANOKE AND WELSH.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND MICROBURST WIND DAMAGE ALONG A 2 MILE LONG BY 300 YD WIDE PATH ABOUT 2 MILES SW OF IOTA. A FEW TREE LIMBS WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG SAFFRON RD. A LARGE TREE WAS UPROOTED AND FELL ON A HOME ON CATTAIL RD AT FABACHER RD WITH DAMAGE TO THE ROOF AND SIDING. ANOTHER LARGE TREE FELL ON A HOME FURTHER SOUTH ON CATTAIL ROAD CRUSHING A VEHICLE AND DAMAGING THE ROOF AND CARPORT. A LARGE TREE WAS BLOWN DOWN ON FABACHER RD JUST WEST OF HWY 91. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AROUND 80 MPH.
SHERIFFS OFFICE RECEIVED REPORTS OF A CARPORT AND VEHICLE DAMAGED ON CATTAIL LANE.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND SEVERE MICROBURST WIND DAMAGE ALONG A 1 MI BY 250 YD PATH ABOUT 1 MILE EAST OF NUBA. A NEW EXIT SIGN ON I-49 WAS BLOWN ONTO THE EAST SERVICE RD. 12 LARGE 1-3 TON STORAGE BLDGS WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED ALONG BAY RIDGE RD AT I-49 WITH DEBRIS BLOWN UP TO 100 YDS AWAY. 3 NEARBY HOMES LOST MANY SHINGLES AND SEVERAL TREES WERE DOWNED ALONG BAY RIDGE RD. LARGE TREE LIMBS WERE BLOWN 200 YDS EAST INTO A POND AT THE WALMART DIST CENTER ON HWY 743. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 90 MPH.
